marketState: neutral-bearish near term with bullish 8-16D heat catalyst and crowded managed-money short position
summary: Front-month NG trades at $3.238, sitting $0.28 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.96 but well within the $2.46–$3.46 band across all near-term horizons. Near-term demand (1-7D) is running 11.9% below the 5y average and fell a sharp 10.2 units in the last 24 hours, which is a direct near-term price headwind; storage is simultaneously 153 Bcf above the 5y norm with the latest injection a 25 Bcf bearish surprise. The pivotal counter-catalyst is the 8-16 day demand outlook: +31.1% above the 5y average and now above the historical maximum range (rangePos 1.43, aboveMax true), an intensification from the prior reading and signalling a major heat event. Managed money remains heavily net short at only the 17th 5y percentile — a crowded position that amplifies squeeze risk if the heat materialises. The winter strip (Dec $4.04, Jan $4.44) anchors a structural bull case for longer-dated positioning.

longTerm.rationale: The 8-16D demand reading has intensified to +31.1% above the 5y average, above the historical maximum range — a rare extreme that should compress the 153 Bcf storage surplus faster than the market currently prices. Managed money at the 17th percentile of 5y net shorts is positioned crowdedly short, creating squeeze fuel once the heat narrative takes hold. Three-week seasonality assigns 69% probability of higher prices, September is the historically strongest month (+3.1%), and the fair-value upper band rises to $3.55 by +21D, leaving technical room from current levels.
longTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $2.95 (through the fair-value midpoint zone), storage surplus widening beyond 200 Bcf above 5y average, or the 8-16D heat forecast revised to near-normal demand levels

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term demand (1-7D) is -11.9% below 5y average with a -10.2 intraday demand drop; the latest storage injection was a 25 Bcf bearish surprise; and short-term technicals (5m/15m) are bearish with RSI at 30–31. Price already sits $0.28 above the fair-value midpoint, which limits the short reward while near-term demand softness limits the long reward. The setup favours waiting for the weak-demand window to clear before positioning ahead of the incoming extreme-heat catalyst.
shortTerm.trigger: Price pulls back to $3.05–$3.12 with short-term RSI turning higher from oversold territory, OR price breaks above $3.32 on above-average volume as the 8-16D above-max heat event begins being actively priced in
